page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-60-lssd is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 60 vCPUs mapped to 30 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 240 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled and consists of 1608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity distributed across four local drives. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (F, VNNI, and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI, which contributes to strong performance in encryption and extended instruction tests. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read bandwidth is strong, whereas uncached large-block read and write bandwidths perform in the average tier. Single-core performance, as measured by Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, is also split between average and strong tiers.

This instance is highly capable for database operations, as evidenced by strong PassMark database and Redis SET operations benchmarks. It is also well-suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and text processing workloads. While it handles small-model LLM inference with strong throughput, performance scales down to average levels for larger models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores, a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, and high-speed local NVMe storage provides a dense resource profile suitable for I/O-intensive workloads and database hosting without requiring specialized storage attachments.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-60-lssd is a general-purpose server configured with 60 vCPUs and 240 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 30 physical cores with dedicated allocation and hyper-threading. The instance includes 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage but lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities, database operations, and cached memory bandwidth, alongside average uncached memory write speeds and single-core performance. The processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, enhancing its execution of complex calculations and encryption tasks. This hardware profile is highly suited for database hosting, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads requiring high-speed local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-60-lssd is a general-purpose server featuring 60 vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-60-lssd features 60 vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and 1608 GB NVMe SSD storage powered by an AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors with high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 60 vCPUs and 240 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 dedicated processors
1608 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Optimized for database hosting, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
